before_model.pattern: No equivalent — legacy system had no orchestrated onboarding flow
before_model.user_creation: Manual multi-step: create user → navigate to profile page → create profile → navigate to roles page → assign roles
before_model.profile_assignment: Separate UI action — no atomic linkage to user creation
before_model.role_assignment: Separate UI action — no atomic linkage to user or profile creation
before_model.transaction_semantics: None — partial state possible (user created, profile not, roles not)
before_model.failure_model: Undefined — partial DB state leaves orphaned users

after_model.pattern: Vertical slice — single SQL transaction wrapping 3 MediatR handler dispatches
after_model.user_creation: Delegates to CreateCustomerUserCommand via IMediator — no logic duplication
after_model.profile_assignment: Delegates to CreateProfileCommand via IMediator — within same transaction
after_model.role_assignment: Delegates to SetUserRoleAccessCommand via IMediator — within same transaction
after_model.transaction_semantics: IDbSession.ExecuteInTransactionAsync wraps all 3 steps — commit on full success, rollback on any step failure
after_model.failure_model: StepFailedException thrown on any !IsSuccess → SQL ROLLBACK → no partial state committed

design_decisions: DD_001: IDbSession.ExecuteInTransactionAsync as outer transaction boundary — all inner handler db.ExecuteAsync calls auto-enrolled (IDbSession is scoped/shared per request)
DD_002: StepFailedException — private signal exception carries Error out of transaction delegate and triggers SQL rollback without propagating as system exception
DD_003: Orchestration via IMediator — inner handlers own all permission checks, validation, SQL; zero logic duplication in orchestrator
DD_004: InitialRoleIds=null on CreateCustomerUserCommand — roles applied atomically in Step 3 (SetUserRoleAccess), not split across two handlers
simplifications: S_001: No saga/compensating transactions — IDbSession shared-scope transaction is sufficient (no distributed system)
S_002: No profile re-use — always creates a new profile for the onboarding user
system_invariants: INV_001: A user onboarded via this endpoint ALWAYS has exactly one profile (CreateProfile required)
INV_002: A user onboarded via this endpoint has exactly the requested roles (SetUserRoleAccess replace-semantics)
INV_003: Either all 3 steps committed, or none — no partial state observable in DB
